variationStrategy.description: Generate variations 5 to 15 focusing on edge cases, trends, and persistent counts.

categories.description: All sub-audits have Stable trends. trendBuckets.increasing and decreasing arrays must be completely empty. Fill only the stable array.
All sub-audits have Increasing trends. trendBuckets.stable and decreasing arrays must be completely empty. Fill only the increasing array.
All sub-audits have Decreasing trends. trendBuckets.stable and increasing arrays must be completely empty. Fill only the decreasing array.
Zero sub-audits breach threshold persistently. persistentTop.totalPersistentCount must be 0 and persistentTop.items must be an empty array [].
Multiple sub-audits (exactly 4) breach the threshold persistently across all schedules. persistentTop.totalPersistentCount is 4 and items contains 4 distinct objects.
The top 3 sub-audits in topByTotalIssues have exactly the same identical totalIssuesAllSchedules and avergeIssuesAllSchedules. Test tie-breaking format.
Exactly 1 previous schedule exists. meta.prevScheduleCount = 1, meta.prevScheduleIds has 1 item, totalScheduleCount = 2.

There are exactly 0 issues in the current schedule. totals.totalIssuesCurrentSchedule = 0. All currentIssueCounts in trends should be 0. All persistentTop items must have issueCount = 0 for the current schedule.
One single sub-audit accounts for 95% of total issues. topByTotalIssues first item has very high contributionPercent, the remaining are minimal (1% or 2%).
Exactly 5 sub-audits exist and all 5 have precisely the same issue counts resulting in 20% contribution each.
All three trend buckets (increasing, decreasing, stable) are filled to their maximum of 3 items each. 9 trend items total.
